mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already low and CPIAUCSL continues to rise, markets infer easier real-rate conditions and stronger risk appetite. That combination pushes investors toward equities and away from duration, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day Treasury-yield changes are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling or below 2% and CPIAUCSL is trending higher, or if the sign flips for most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals fast labor-market deterioration, which increases recession odds and bid demand for Treasuries. Under that threshold, the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-crossing episodes are followed by rising 10-year yields more often than falling yields, or if PAYEMS does not weaken alongside the UNRATE jump.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates household spending and housing demand are accelerating. That improves growth expectations and lifts term premia, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS or HOUST improve in isolation but the combined signal does not produce positive 20-day yield changes, or if the joint effect is weaker than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO contracts while GDP growth has already slowed, the market reads an industrial recession signal and seeks duration protection. This pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO momentum is not followed by lower 10-year yields, or if the relationship disappears once GDP growth is already accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A federal-funds rate at or above 4.5% signals a tightening regime in which discount rates and real yields move higher. In that regime, the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice policy restraint.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS episodes do not produce positive 5-day yield changes, or if the signal reverses and yields fall persistently after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating faster than price data can offset. That recession-risk combination increases demand for safe assets, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS weakness and UNRATE strength do not predict lower 20-day yields, or if the effect is absent when the two series move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising RSAFS together with rising HOUST signals renewed private-sector demand and firmer nominal growth. That broadens inflation and growth expectations, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined demand-recovery signal fails to raise 60-day yields, or if the effect is concentrated only in episodes without CPIAUCSL acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production weakens at the same time employment growth stalls, the market infers a synchronized real-economy slowdown rather than a temporary noise shock. That drives safe-haven demand and lowers the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weakness in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not precede lower 5-day yields, or if the signal is not materially stronger than single-series effects.
